description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly light gray to off-white coloration, featuring subtle patches of darker gray and occasional embedded pebbles. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible pitting, crevices, and a few angular protrusions, suggesting natural weathering. One side displays a more pronounced cavity or indentation, while the rest of the rock is more rounded and compact. The texture appears gritty and hard, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. The rock's mass and density are consistent with typical urban landscaping stones, and it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of human modification on its surface.
description: A rough, light gray rock with an irregular, weathered surface and embedded pebbles. It features a mix of rounded and angular forms, with a gritty, hard texture.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, highlighting its rounded top and a subtle angular ridge on the right side.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a deeper cavity and more pronounced crevices, with some darker gray inclusions visible.
description_view_2: Here, the rock appears more compact and rounded, with fewer visible indentations and a smoother overall contour.
description_view_3: This side displays a narrow, elongated profile with a mostly continuous, rough surface and minimal protrusions.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
